1	# WebIde
2	
3	A competitive coding web IDE and evaluator — think LeetCode, but self-hosted and built from scratch as a learning project. Users submit code, it runs in an isolated sandbox, and results are streamed back in real time.
4	
5	## Status
6	
7	Early development. The domain model layer (Lab-1) is complete. The web application (ASP.NET MVC backend + React/TypeScript frontend) is next.
8	
9	## Planned Stack
10	
11	| Layer | Technology |
12	|---|---|
13	| Frontend | React + TypeScript (Vite dev, Nginx prod) |
14	| Backend | ASP.NET MVC (.NET 10) |
15	| Real-time | SignalR |
16	| Database | PostgreSQL |
17	| Cache / Sessions | Redis |
18	| Code Execution | Isolated sandbox (Docker) |
19	| Reverse Proxy | Nginx |
20	
21	## Project Structure
22	
23	```
24	WebIde.Model/       — Domain model: classes, enums, relationships
25	WebIde.Console/     — Lab-1 console app: data population, LINQ queries, async demo
26	docs/               — Architecture and design documentation
27	lab-1/              — AI agent usage logs (course requirement)
28	```
29	
30	## Running the Console App (Lab-1)
31	
32	Requires [.NET 10 SDK](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download).
33	
34	```bash
35	dotnet run --project WebIde.Console
36	```
37	
38	## Documentation
39	
40	- [Architecture](docs/architecture.md) — system design, layers, scaling plan
41	- [Domain Model](docs/domain-model.md) — all classes, enums, and their relationships
42

content.text: Perfect! Now let me check if there's any documentation and get a complete picture:

content.content:
1	# Architecture
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	WebIde is a self-hosted competitive coding platform. Users write code in a browser-based editor, submit it, and get results streamed back in real time. The system separates concerns into distinct layers: presentation, business logic, and code execution.
6	
7	## Current State
8	
9	The domain model layer (`WebIde.Model`) is implemented as a .NET class library. A console app (`WebIde.Console`) exercises the model with LINQ queries and async patterns. No web layer exists yet.
10	
11	## Target Architecture
12	
13	```
14	Browser
15	  └── React/TypeScript SPA
16	        │
17	        │ HTTP /api/*
18	        ▼
19	  ASP.NET MVC (.NET 10)          ← controllers, services, EF Core
20	        │
21	        ├── PostgreSQL            ← persistent storage
22	        ├── Redis                 ← sessions, caching
23	        └── Sandbox Worker        ← isolated code execution (Docker)
24	              │
25	              │ SignalR
26	              ▼
27	        Browser (real-time execution output stream)
28	```
29	
30	Nginx sits in front of everything as a reverse proxy:
31	- `/app/*` → React frontend (Nginx static in prod, Vite dev server in dev)
32	- `/api/*` → ASP.NET MVC backend
33	
34	## Layers
35	
36	### WebIde.Model
37	Pure C# class library. No dependencies on any framework. Contains:
38	- Domain entity classes (Problem, Submission, User, etc.)
39	- Enums (DifficultyLevel, SubmissionStatus, UserRole)
40	
41	This library is referenced by any layer that needs to work with the domain — backend API, tests, etc.
42	
43	### Backend (planned)
44	ASP.NET MVC (.NET 10). Responsibilities:
45	- REST API for the frontend
46	- Business logic (submission scoring, leaderboard updates)
47	- EF Core ORM talking to PostgreSQL
48	- SignalR hub for streaming execution output
49	
50	### Frontend (planned)
51	React + TypeScript, bundled with Vite. Responsibilities:
52	- Code editor (Monaco Editor)
53	- Problem browser, submission history, leaderboard
54	- SignalR client for real-time output
55	
56	### Sandbox (planned)
57	Isolated execution environment for untrusted code. Each submission gets:
58	- A container with strict CPU, memory, and time limits
59	- No network access
60	- Results (stdout, stderr, exit code, wall time, peak memory) written back to the backend
61	
62	## Scaling Plan
63	
64	| Stage | Users | Infrastructure |
65	|---|---|---|
66	| 1 | ~100 | Single VPS (4 vCPU / 8 GB), 2 sandbox runners |
67	| 2 | ~500 | App node + dedicated worker node, managed PostgreSQL |
68	| 3+ | 5 000+ | Kubernetes, auto-scaling worker pools, read replicas |
69	
70	The bottleneck at scale is the sandbox, not the API or database. C/C++ submissions take ~1s compile + ~2s run, so sandbox runners need dedicated CPU for deterministic timing.
71

content.text: Excellent! Let me get one more view of the repositories to understand the data layer:

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive report.

## Project Exploration Summary

I've thoroughly explored the WebIde project. Here's what I found:

### 1. **Overall Project Structure**

The project is organized into separate .NET projects:

| Project | Path | Type | Purpose |
|---------|------|------|---------|
| **WebIde.Model** | `/WebIde.Model/` | Class Library (.NET 10) | Domain model — pure C# classes, no framework dependencies |
| **WebIde.Console** | `/WebIde.Console/` | Console App (.NET 10) | Lab-1 exercise — data population, LINQ queries, async demos |
| **WebIde.Frontend** | `/WebIde.Frontend/` | ASP.NET MVC Web App (.NET 10) | Lab-2 in progress — MVC controllers, views, mock repositories |
| **WebIde.Api** | `/WebIde.Api/` | (Empty) | Planned future API project |

**Key Files:**
- `/README.md` — project overview and stack info
- `/docs/architecture.md` — system design and scaling plan
- `/docs/domain-model.md` — entity relationships

### 2. **App Type**

**ASP.NET MVC (Lab-2)** with planned evolution:
- Currently: ASP.NET MVC with in-memory mock repositories (no database)
- Planned: REST API backend + React/TypeScript SPA frontend
- The WebIde.Frontend project is an MVC web app with 8 controllers (Home, Problem, User, Submission, Organization, ProblemSet, Tag, Leaderboard) serving server-rendered Razor views
- React/TypeScript frontend is not yet implemented; current views are traditional Razor templates

### 3. **Database Configuration**

**Current Status: No Database Configured (Lab-2)**
- Using in-memory **mock repositories** with static seed data
- No EF Core DbContext implemented
- No migrations exist
- No connection strings in appsettings

**Mock Data Location:** `/WebIde.Frontend/Repositories/MockData.cs` — static in-memory graph with hardcoded problems, users, organizations, tags, problem sets, and submissions

**Planned (Post-Lab-2):**
- PostgreSQL database
- EF Core ORM
- Redis for sessions/caching
- Connection strings will go in appsettings

### 4. **How to Run the App**

**WebIde.Frontend (MVC App):**
```bash
cd /Users/fbeleta/Documents/Web-ide/Web_ide
dotnet run --project WebIde.Frontend
```
- **HTTP:** `http://localhost:5197`
- **HTTPS:** `https://localhost:7001`
- Launch profiles: http, https (both in Development mode)
- Configuration: `/WebIde.Frontend/Properties/launchSettings.json`
- App settings: `/WebIde.Frontend/appsettings.json` and `appsettings.Development.json`

**WebIde.Console (Lab-1 Demo):**
```bash
dotnet run --project WebIde.Console
```

**No Makefile or scripts** — standard .NET CLI only.

### 5. **Existing Migrations**

**None.** No migrations directory exists, no DbContext is configured, and no database infrastructure is set up. This is expected for Lab-2 in early development with mock repositories.

### 6. **Domain Model Overview**

**Core Entities** (in `/WebIde.Model/`):
- `User` — username, email, display name, role, registered date
- `Problem` — title, description, difficulty, time/memory limits, test cases
- `Submission` — tracks user code submissions and execution status
- `TestCase` — input/output pairs with sample flag and point value
- `Tag` — problem categorization (Arrays, Hash Map, Sliding Window, etc.)
- `Organization` — groups users and problems
- `ProblemSet` — collections of problems
- `ExecutionResult` — output from sandbox execution

**Enums:**
- `DifficultyLevel` — Easy, Medium, Hard
- `SubmissionStatus` — Pending, Running, Accepted, WrongAnswer, TimeLimitExceeded, MemoryLimitExceeded, CompileError
- `UserRole` — Admin, Instructor, Student

### 7. **Key Project Characteristics**

- **Framework:** .NET 10, C# with nullable reference types enabled
- **Frontend:** Currently server-rendered Razor views (MVC); React/TypeScript planned
- **Real-time:** SignalR planned for streaming code execution output
- **Architecture:** Layered — Model → Controllers → Mock Repositories (→ eventual DB)
- **Repositories:** 6 mock repositories (Problem, User, Organization, ProblemSet, Submission, Tag) backed by static in-memory MockData

**Related Directories:**
- `/design-files/` — UI/UX design assets
- `/lab-1/` — AI agent usage logs (course requirement)
- `/lab-2/` — current phase work
- `/docs/` — architecture and domain model documentation
